methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public-stage company operating in Climate & Energy, having raised approximately C$100M to date. The comparable universe for this sector comprises 21 peer companies, though the target's specific positioning yields limited narrow-peer matches—only 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers identified, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The peer-median triangulation method is inapplicable in this case. Despite 21 same-sector peers existing in Climate & Energy, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints across each filter. A median derived from all 21 sector peers would introduce substantial methodological noise given the heterogeneity in company maturity, capital structures, and technology readiness across the broader Climate & Energy landscape. Consequently, traditional peer-multiple approaches cannot reliably anchor a valuation range.

Given data constraints, valuation support must rely on alternative methods not detailed in this dataset. The identified private peer set—particularly later-stage, well-capitalized fusion and advanced-materials companies—may offer directional context, but lacks sufficient homogeneity to serve as a primary valuation lever. Key uncertainties center on the availability of revenue, EBITDA, or R&D spending comparables within the peer set, as well as the applicability of any venture-stage or later-stage capital raise multiples to a public trading entity. Additional financial metrics from LNZA and the private peer cohort would be required to establish a defensible range.
